old_claim.note: Early Alchemy & Material Science is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: No direct early-alchemy dependency remains because the dyed-textile node is scoped to coloring fibers and cloth, which can be represented through textile production without a broad proto-chemistry ancestor.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica's textile article supports textiles and coloring/dyeing practice as textile production context, not as a dependency on alchemical material science.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports dyed textiles through textile practice rather than an early alchemy/material-science prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Alchemy is too broad and laterally related; keeping textiles_weaving direct preserves the material substrate without laundering chemistry history into dyeing.

ontology_before: Dyed Textiles depended on Early Alchemy & Material Science as an unsourced historical predecessor.
ontology_after: Dyed Textiles depends directly on Textiles & Weaving; chemistry-specific dye developments can be represented on later synthetic-dye nodes.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: early_alchemy_material_science is no longer a direct prerequisite for dyed_textiles.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to mordant chemistry rather than dyed textile production broadly.
A stronger source showed early alchemical material science directly preceded the first dyed textiles.
A separate mordant_dye_chemistry node were added and needed early alchemy as a scoped predecessor.
short_reason: Broad proto-chemistry is not required for dyed textiles.
